items.title: Stage a proposal — captures live before-state, persists pending, tenant-scoped
items.precondition: Super-admin session; a website project with a page carrying seo_data.
steps: 1. POST /api/admin/websites/:id/seo/metadata-proposals with { pageId, proposedTitle, proposedDescription, rationale }.
items.expected: 201 with a pending row whose before_title/before_description come from the page's live seo_data; a pageId outside the project returns 404 PAGE_NOT_FOUND.
items.evidence: Layer 1: src/__tests__/page-metadata-proposals.test.ts — stageProposal suite (live before-state, null before-state, EMPTY_PROPOSAL, tenant-scoped PAGE_NOT_FOUND) all pass.
items.notes: Layer-1 verified. Layer-2 (live HTTP) pending — needs the running app.

items.title: Approve records an approval; reject closes it; review is idempotent
items.precondition: A pending proposal id from T1.
items.expected: First call returns the row at status=approved with reviewed_by set as a JSON NUMBER (not "7"); second call returns 409 PROPOSAL_ALREADY_REVIEWED; an unknown id returns 404 PROPOSAL_NOT_FOUND. No live page changes (publishing is a separate step).
items.evidence: Layer 1: page-metadata-proposals.test.ts — approve/reject suite (approve records reviewer, reject, PROPOSAL_NOT_FOUND, 409 PROPOSAL_ALREADY_REVIEWED) all pass. reviewed_by is proven numeric by page-metadata-proposals-contract.test.ts, and the column is declared integer (not bigInteger, which node-postgres returns as a string).
items.notes: Layer-1 verified. Layer-2 (live HTTP) pending — needs the running app.

items.title: Boundary validation rejects malformed input
steps: 1. POST .../metadata-proposals with a non-uuid pageId or an empty proposedTitle.
items.expected: 400 VALIDATION_ERROR on a bad body, a bad ?status, AND a non-uuid :id or :proposalId route param (schemas are .strict(), enforce mode, applied to body, query, and params).
items.evidence: Layer 1: page-metadata-proposals-contract.test.ts — the params schemas reject a non-uuid :id and :proposalId, all four routes carry a params-target validate(), and the rationale bounds reject an over-key and multi-megabyte blob.
items.notes: Layer-1 verified at the schema and route-wiring level. Layer-2 (a live 400 over HTTP) still pending — needs the running app.

items.title: Every response uses the §8.1 contract
steps: 1. POST .../metadata-proposals (success) and inspect the JSON body.
2. POST .../metadata-proposals/:proposalId/approve twice and inspect the 409 body.
items.expected: Success is exactly { success: true, data, error: null }. Failure is exactly { success: false, data: null, error: { code, message, details } } — never `error` as a bare string, never a sibling top-level `message`.
items.evidence: Layer 1: page-metadata-proposals-contract.test.ts — the §8.1 suite asserts both shapes exactly and that an unrecognized driver error becomes a generic 500 with no internal detail (§3.4).
items.notes: These four endpoints deliberately do NOT use the domain's older util.integration-responses helper, which emits a non-§8.1 shape. Layer-2 (live HTTP) pending.

items.title: Approving changes no live page
items.precondition: A pending proposal and its page id.
steps: 1. Record SELECT seo_data FROM website_builder.pages WHERE id = :pageId.
items.expected: Byte-identical seo_data. This is the claim the whole scope boundary rests on.
items.notes: Layer 1 proves the service only calls PageMetadataProposalModel on the review path; a live read/write comparison needs the running app.
